data.title: Volvo Cars sales rise 5% Y/Y in November
data.description:
• Volvo Cars (OTCPK:VOLAF) saw a 5% Y/Y rise to 66,977 cars in its global sales in November, boosted by robust demand in Europe and the U.S..
  • However, the company saw a drop in sales in China.
  • The company's sale of electrified models remained strong, and grew 40% as compared to the same period last year.
  • The share of fully electric cars constituted 21% of all cars sold for the month, the firm reported.
  • In Europe, sales were up 20% Y/Y, while U.S. saw an increase of 5%.
  • Volvo Cars' sales in China dropped 8% Y/Y.
  • The Volvo XC60 was the top-selling model in November.

data.title: Athena Gold upsizes private placement from C$1M to up to C$1.25M
data.description:
• Athena Gold (OTCQB:AHNR) has increased the size of its previously announced non-brokered private placement from C$1M to up to C$1.25M.
  • The offering will now consist of up to 25M units (increased from 20M units). The units are priced at C$0.05 per unit.
  • The other terms of the offering, announced on Oct. 3, remain unchanged.
  • Additionally, the company said it has closed the second tranche of the offering through the issuance of 6.46M units for gross proceeds of C$323K.
  • The first tranche of the offering closed involved the issuance of 12M units for gross proceeds of C$600K.
  • The proceeds from the offering will be used to fund exploration work on the company's Excelsior Springs Project located in Nevada, USA, and the Laird Lake and Oneman Lake Projects recently acquired in Ontario, Canada, and for general and administrative expenses.
  • Any surplus will be used to provide general working capital and additional exploration.
  • AHNR shares were trading -24.37% Wednesday pre-market at $0.03.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Lucara Diamond sees 2025 diamond revenue between $195M – $225M
data.description:
• Lucara Diamond (TSX:LUC:CA) says in Karowe Mine for the full year 2025 it expects diamond revenue to be between $195M – $225M.
  • In 2025, the company expects to mine between 1.8 million and 2.2 million tonnes of combined ore and water.
  • Sustaining capital are expected to be up to $13 million.
  • The firm said it expects 2025 total cash operating costs to be $28.50 – $31 per tonne processed.

data.title: Manulife Financial proposes $1B debentures offering
data.description:
• Manulife Financial (NYSE:MFC) intends to issue $1B principal amount of 4.064% fixed/floating subordinated debentures due Dec 6, 2034.
  • The debentures will bear interest at a fixed rate of 4.064% until Dec 6, 2029 and thereafter at 1.25% over Daily Compounded CORRA.
  • Subject to prior regulatory approval, MFC may redeem the debentures, in whole or in part, on or after Dec. 6, 2029 at a price equal to par, together with accrued and unpaid interest to, but excluding, the date fixed for redemption.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from the offering for general corporate purposes, including investment in subsidiaries and potential future redemptions of existing securities.

data.title: Alset AI Ventures names Jonathan Yan as CFO
data.description:
• Alset AI Ventures (OTCPK:ALSCF) said it has appointed Jonathan Yan as its new finance chief.
  • Yan was previously the CFO of Axcap Ventures.

data.title: indie semiconductor prices note offering
data.description:
• indie Semiconductor (NASDAQ:INDI) has priced its upsized $190 million aggregate principal amount of its 3.50% convertible senior notes due 2029
  • Underwriters has also granted the initial purchasers in the offering an option to purchase, an additional $28.5 million aggregate principal amount of notes.
  • Closing date is December 6, 2024.
  • The notes will be senior unsecured obligations of indie, and interest on the notes will be payable semiannually in arrears, beginning on June 15, 2025. The notes will mature on December 15, 2029, unless earlier repurchased, redeemed or converted.
  • $20.3M out of the total net proceeds of approximately $183.3 million (or approximately $210.9 million if the initial purchasers exercise their option to purchase additional notes in full) will be used to pay the cost of the capped call transactions.
  • The notes will be convertible into cash, shares of common stock or a combination of cash and shares of common stock, at indie’s election, at an initial conversion rate of 194.6188 shares of common stock per $1,000 principal amount of notes, which is equivalent to an initial conversion price of approximately $5.14 per share of common stock. The initial conversion price of the notes represents a premium of approximately 27.5% to the $4.03 per share last reported sale price.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: MarketAxess reports trading volume statistics for November 2024
data.description:
MarketAxess (MKTX) announced total average daily volume (ADV) of $44.9B for November, up 56% Y/Y and +3% compared to October 2024, driven by a rebound in U.S. Treasury ADV.

Total rates ADV reached $30.7 billion, showing a 112% increase from the previous year.

Total credit ADV of $14.3 billion was in line with the prior year, but decreased 5% compared to October 2024.

The preliminary FPM for total credit for November 2024 was approximately $146, down from $157 in the prior year, and down from $154 in October 2024.

Preliminary total credit FPM quarter-to-date through November 30 was approximately $151 compared to $149 in third quarter 2024.

The preliminary FPM for total rates for November 2024 was approximately $4.07, down from $5.07 in the prior year, and down from $4.40 in October 2024.

Notable achievements include record emerging markets ADV of $3.8 billion (up 15% Y/Y) and record municipal bond ADV of $631 million (up 5% Y/Y).

The company's portfolio trading solution showed positive engagement with 21 dealers and 26 long-only clients, with average trade size over $4 million notional.

A record 76% of portfolio trading volume was executed over X-Pro.

Shares of MarketAxess declined 0.64% in premarket on Wednesday.

data.title: Daktronics beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; reaffirms FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Daktronics press release (NASDAQ:DAKT): Q2 GAAP EPS of $0.22 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $208.33M (+4.5% Y/Y) beats by $5.74M.
  • Record cash flows from operations of $43.3 million for the fiscal second quarter and $62.8 million for the first six months of fiscal 2025.
  • Product order backlog of $236.0 million at October 26, 2024, compared to $267.2 million at the end of the first quarter of fiscal 2025 and $306.9 million at the end of the second quarter of fiscal 2024.
  • Product and service orders of $177.6 million for the quarter, a decrease of 3.3 percent from the second quarter of 2024 and $353.8 million on a year-to-date basis, a year-to-date increase of 3.3 percent.
  • Outlook: Daktronics enters the third quarter of fiscal 2025 with order growth of 3.3 percent year-over-year, and continues to expect order volume to increase for the full fiscal year. The first half of the fiscal year is typically the highest-volume sales of the year, and are expected to be at normal "seasonal" levels for the remainder of fiscal 2025.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Chewy raises FY2024 outlook after mixed FQ3 results
Eli Lilly’s Zepbound outperforms Novo's Wegovy for weight loss in head-to-head trial
data.description:
Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) said Wednesday its weight-loss drug Zepbound (tirzepatide) provided a 47% greater relative weight loss compared to Danish rival Novo Nordisk’s (NVO) Wegovy (semaglutide) in the first head-to-head trial of the two blockbuster weight loss drugs.

In a study sponsored by Lilly, people taking Zepbound lost an average of 20% of their body weight, while those on Wegovy shed 14%. Specifically, participants using Zepbound lost 50.3 lbs (22.8 kg) and participants on Wegovy lost 33.1 lbs (15.0 kg).

At 72 weeks, Zepbound beat Wegovy on both the primary endpoint and all five key secondary endpoints in this 751-person trial (SURMOUNT-5 phase 3b) of adults living with obesity or overweight with at least one weight-related medical problem and without diabetes.

In a key secondary endpoint, 31.6% of people taking Zepbound achieved at least 25% body weight loss compared to 16.1% of those taking Wegovy.

The full results will be published in a peer-reviewed journal and presented at a medical meeting next year, Lilly said.

Tirzepatide is commercialized for adults with obesity or with overweight who also have weight-related medical problems as Zepbound in the U.S. and Mounjaro in some global markets outside the U.S. The drug is also commercialized as Mounjaro for adults with type 2 diabetes in the U.S.

Semaglutide is commercialized as Wegovy for people living with obesity or for adults with overweight who also have weight-related medical problems and Ozempic for people with type 2 diabetes.

U.S.-listed shares of Novo Nordisk (NVO) dropped marginally premarket. Lilly (LLY) shares rose as much as 2% in trading before U.S. markets opened.
